elder.name: Elder
elder.dialogue: The Elder watches the roads like someone counting losses before they happen, because in a small village every journey changes more than one life.

merchant.name: Merchant
merchant.dialogue: The Merchant keeps one eye on stock, one eye on the door, and enough patience to treat coin, rumor, and danger as parts of the same trade.

scout.name: Scout
scout.dialogue: The Scout reads the road in snapped twigs and trampled mud, as if every passing threat signed its own report.

caretaker.name: Caretaker
caretaker.dialogue: The Caretaker handles every cracked rune as if care itself might be the only thing still holding the shrine's history together.

captain.name: Captain
captain.dialogue: The Captain speaks like someone who has no time for ornament and too much experience with small threats turning into funerals.

ferryman.name: Ferryman
ferryman.dialogue: The Ferryman remembers every face that boards in haste, every cart that creaks too heavily, and every crossing people later wish they had delayed.

scholar.name: Scholar
scholar.dialogue: The Scholar treats the ruins like a wounded archive, convinced the old stones still say useful things if someone listens carefully enough.

hermit.name: Hermit
hermit.dialogue: The Hermit trusts cave echoes more than most travelers, insisting stone lies less often than frightened people do.

traveler.name: Traveler
traveler.dialogue: The Traveler looks like someone who has already spent too much pride staying on their feet this long.

gatewarden.name: Gatewarden
gatewarden.dialogue: The Gatewarden checks wagons, faces, and weather with the same patient care, because Stonewatch stays calm only if its threshold stays watched.

reactions.text: The Gatewarden squares his shoulders the moment he sees you. "Stonewatch has not forgotten the Gorgos-Slayer. The pass still stands easier because of that kill."
The Gatewarden's posture shifts the moment he recognizes you. "You're the one who slew Gorgos and kept Ironridge's line intact."
The Gatewarden offers a crisp nod. "D-rank and steady work. Stonewatch notices."
With your Kingdom Guard standing known, the gate checks feel more like formality than suspicion.

rumors.text: Ironridge keeps the mountain line open, but the pass only truly relaxes after Gorgos the Sundered stops owning it.

market_broker.name: Market Broker
market_broker.dialogue: The Market Broker tracks shortages, caravan delays, and salvage prices with the calm of someone who knows town coin moves on rumor almost as often as goods.

reactions.text: The broker has started filing your work under proven hands rather than hopeful drifters.
With Merchant Guild standing behind your name, the broker speaks as if you belong on the square's serious side of the ledger.

rumors.text: Wolfpack Culling still pays because Ashwood packs keep testing the line whenever the road looks soft.
Blackroot Ash Mark is the sort of contract people whisper about before they earn the right to read the posting.

blacksmith.name: Blacksmith
blacksmith.dialogue: The Blacksmith speaks like steel ought to be honest, with no patience for flashy junk and plenty for people who mean to survive what they buy or bring in for work.

arcanist.name: Arcanist
arcanist.dialogue: The Arcanist arranges powders, glass, and warded tools with exact care, treating reagents, tonics, and focus pieces as things that punish sloppy buyers.

fletcher.name: Fletcher
fletcher.dialogue: The Fletcher talks about balance, reach, and timing with the dry certainty of someone who expects customers to know what kind of road they are paying for.

innkeeper.name: Innkeeper
innkeeper.dialogue: The Innkeeper keeps a warm room, cheap food, and enough discretion to let road-worn strangers feel human again before morning, so long as they pay before they vanish.

rumors.text: Stormbreak's ferries still run, but only fools mistake a paid berth for a safe coast.
Emberfall sends people on under ward and ledger, and Vaultreach sends back only the ones the line did not break.

quartermaster.name: Quartermaster
quartermaster.dialogue: The Quartermaster keeps Stonewatch's watch supplied with plain gear, sharper records, and a suspicion of anyone who wastes either, especially on the town's coin.

shrine_caretaker.name: Shrine Caretaker
shrine_caretaker.dialogue: The Shrine Caretaker keeps the little town altar swept, lit, and useful, insisting that watched flames, clean ash, and steady hands matter as much as prayer does.

reactions.text: The Shrine Caretaker bows their head. "You carried the sigil home. That is what keeping the realm's duty looks like."
Your Shrine Keepers standing has clearly changed the tone here from caution to shared duty.

rumors.text: Emberfall still holds because people there keep choosing ward-duty over comfort, especially when the ash roads grow louder.

aldric.name: Aldric
aldric.dialogue: Aldric counts losses with a trader's calm and a survivor's memory, talking about roads, raiders, and recovered cargo as if all three belong on the same ledger line.

dialogue_set.default: Aldric counts losses with a trader's calm and a survivor's memory, talking about roads, raiders, and recovered cargo as if all three belong on the same ledger line.

aldric.lore: Aldric keeps trading because he thinks a frontier road that still carries honest freight is proof Valerion has not yielded yet.

reactions.text: Aldric gives you the kind of look reserved for people who have already proven they can keep trade alive instead of merely profit from it.
Aldric taps the counter once. "You brought merchant work back across a bad road. That kind of steadiness buys more than coin."

hold_warden.name: Hold Warden
hold_warden.dialogue: The Hold Warden speaks like a road ledger with a spear behind it, counting caravans, raiders, and broken axle stories with equal care.

reactions.text: The Hold Warden now treats you like someone cleared for harder convoy work on the mountain line.
There is no reserve left in the Warden's voice when Gorgos comes up; the pass remembers who finally brought that brute down.

rumors.text: No caravan master forgets Gorgos the Sundered for long; the pass tolls itself in broken wheels until someone answers him properly.

thane_brom.name: Thane Brom
thane_brom.dialogue: Thane Brom carries himself like someone used to weighing people by what they finish, not by how loudly they promise it.

dialogue_set.default: Thane Brom carries himself like someone used to weighing people by what they finish, not by how loudly they promise it.

thane_brom.lore: Brom treats Ironridge as a duty measured in stone, convoy law, and the refusal to let mountain roads become raider country.

reactions.text: Brom studies you in silence before nodding once. "A road is only a road when someone keeps monsters from naming it theirs. Ironridge remembers Gorgos."
Thane Brom's tone turns practical rather than testing. "C-rank means the hold can speak plainly to you."

ledger_clerk.name: Ledger Clerk
ledger_clerk.dialogue: The Ledger Clerk keeps slate, ink, and caravan tallies moving in steady order, treating supplies as seriously as soldiers treat arrows.

rumors.text: Restricted vault transit does not care how impatient you are; the books only open after rank and Blackroot Ash Mark both say you belong there.

forgemistress.name: Hesta Runehand
forgemistress.dialogue: The Forge-Mistress judges gear by balance, temper, and whether it comes back bent, which she considers a confession rather than an accident.

dialogue_set.default: The Forge-Mistress judges gear by balance, temper, and whether it comes back bent, which she considers a confession rather than an accident.

forgemistress.lore: Hesta Runehand treats every repaired shield and straightened edge as part of the same work of keeping Ironridge's line from failing under strain.

reactions.text: Hesta grunts in approval. "Steel lasts longer when someone finally teaches the mountain who it belongs to. Gorgos proved that."
Hesta treats you less like a passing buyer and more like someone worth fitting properly for the road.

coast_ranger.name: Coast Ranger
coast_ranger.dialogue: The Coast Ranger watches surf, tracks, and broken rigging with the same hard patience, because trouble on the shore almost always arrives in pieces first.

reactions.text: Stormbreak's watch clearly knows you have taken the coastward line seriously enough to deserve plain answers.

rumors.text: Stormbreak Cove looks like broken timber and easy salvage until the raiders decide you are part of the wreckage.

vault_scholar.name: Scholar Ilya
vault_scholar.dialogue: The Vault Scholar studies soot marks and cult sigils with unnerving calm, intent on learning what the ash-vault once kept before the wrong people finish claiming it.

dialogue_set.default: The Vault Scholar studies soot marks and cult sigils with unnerving calm, intent on learning what the ash-vault once kept before the wrong people finish claiming it.

vault_scholar.lore: Ilya works the vault ledgers because someone has to remember what the relics were for before cult ash and fear rewrite the record.

reactions.text: Ilya's reserve breaks for a moment. "Then the Vault Keeper truly fell. Good. Some lines can be studied only after someone survives them."

rumors.text: Vaultreach only looks calm because the warded line is doing its work every hour you are not down in the dark seeing it strain.

guild_broker_maren.name: Broker Maren
guild_broker_maren.dialogue: Broker Maren keeps contract notices, freight letters, and rumor slips under the same steady hand, convinced the realm survives when information reaches the right person before panic does.

dialogue_set.default: Broker Maren keeps contract notices, freight letters, and rumor slips under the same steady hand, convinced the realm survives when information reaches the right person before panic does.

guild_broker_maren.lore: Maren runs guild paperwork like frontier triage, sorting which dangers become jobs and which jobs become the realm holding one more week.

reactions.text: Maren taps your name on the ledger with a dry smile. "Gorgos-Slayer means I can stop pretending you need the easy postings explained."
Maren glances down the ledger and then back up with a different kind of interest. "Bronze standing with the Hunter Guild changes which postings I bother pointing out."
Maren no longer introduces you to the work like a stranger; she speaks as if your name already belongs on the frontier rolls.

rumors.text: If you want the realm to remember your name, take contracts that keep other people sleeping through the night.

veteran_hunter_sera.name: Sera Vale
veteran_hunter_sera.dialogue: Sera Vale speaks like someone who has buried more beasts than trophies, measuring every trail by what it costs the people who live nearby.

dialogue_set.default: Sera Vale speaks like someone who has buried more beasts than trophies, measuring every trail by what it costs the people who live nearby.

veteran_hunter_sera.lore: Sera teaches that monster work is border duty first and glory never, because every beast left to rule a path teaches the realm to shrink.

reactions.text: Sera studies you like a peer now that the Ashwood alpha hunt has your mark on it.

rumors.text: Alpha on Ashwood Trail pays because one smart beast can thin a road faster than a dozen loud fools.
If you want a real challenge, bring down the Ashwood Fanglord and come back standing.

shrine_archivist_elira.name: Archivist Elira
shrine_archivist_elira.dialogue: Archivist Elira handles every copied ward and soot-marked register like a surviving oath, certain that written memory is part of how Valerion keeps its line from failing twice.

dialogue_set.default: Archivist Elira handles every copied ward and soot-marked register like a surviving oath, certain that written memory is part of how Valerion keeps its line from failing twice.

shrine_archivist_elira.lore: Elira believes archive work is frontier work, because a realm that loses the reason for its wards rarely keeps the wards themselves for long.

reactions.text: Elira's manner softens once she knows you returned the sigil instead of treating it like salvage.

rumors.text: Silence the Weeping Shrine if you mean to prove you can keep ward-duty without flinching when the old places answer back.
A relic core shard would let us finish the cipher and keep the ward math from drifting.

caravan_master_dain.name: Master Dain
caravan_master_dain.dialogue: Master Dain talks in distances, axle strain, and oaths kept at the next gate, treating every caravan as one more answer to the question of whether the realm still holds together.

dialogue_set.default: Master Dain talks in distances, axle strain, and oaths kept at the next gate, treating every caravan as one more answer to the question of whether the realm still holds together.

caravan_master_dain.lore: Dain trusts roads only after they have held under load, which is why he treats every safe arrival as a civic duty rather than a business success.

reactions.text: Dain stops explaining the roads like you are new to them once your board rank proves you have paid enough miles into the line.

rumors.text: Waycarter lines are not shortcuts; they are the realm spending coin and discipline so the roads stay worth having.

road_captain_ves.name: Captain Ves
road_captain_ves.dialogue: Captain Ves watches traffic like a battlefield quartermaster, certain that a held line fails first in the details people wave aside as small.

dialogue_set.default: Captain Ves watches traffic like a battlefield quartermaster, certain that a held line fails first in the details people wave aside as small.

road_captain_ves.lore: Ves treats road command as the art of making danger arrive too late to matter, one convoy schedule and patrol correction at a time.

reactions.text: Ves addresses you as working company now that your rank says you can be trusted where the road turns costly.
After Gorgos fell, Ves stopped speaking of the pass as a wound that had to stay open and started speaking of it as a line that might hold again.

rumors.text: The harder contracts are not glory work; they are what keep ordinary wagons ordinary instead of memorials.

merchant_factor_celene.name: Factor Celene
merchant_factor_celene.dialogue: Factor Celene speaks like every shipment has two prices: the coin on the page and the cost if the road fails before dawn.

dialogue_set.default: Factor Celene speaks like every shipment has two prices: the coin on the page and the cost if the road fails before dawn.

merchant_factor_celene.lore: Celene keeps Stonewatch and Stormbreak trade aligned because she thinks frontier economies collapse one missing convoy at a time.

reactions.text: Celene flips straight to practical terms. "Good. A known name means I can skip the warnings and get to the routes that matter."

forest_scout_rowan.name: Scout Rowan
forest_scout_rowan.dialogue: Scout Rowan speaks softly enough that the woods seem to lean in, giving every trail report the weight of a warning worth surviving.

dialogue_set.default: Scout Rowan speaks softly enough that the woods seem to lean in, giving every trail report the weight of a warning worth surviving.

forest_scout_rowan.lore: Rowan serves the clan paths as if every animal trail and warded bridge were part of the same frontier oath.

reactions.text: Rowan no longer measures every word before giving it to you. The Forest Clans have already judged your tread.

rumors.text: If you are heading toward the old watch line, keep an eye out for a missing hunter signal.

harbor_master_oren.name: Harbor Master Oren
harbor_master_oren.dialogue: Harbor Master Oren watches ropes, manifests, and weather with the calm of someone who knows a coast falls apart first in its bookkeeping.

dialogue_set.default: Harbor Master Oren watches ropes, manifests, and weather with the calm of someone who knows a coast falls apart first in its bookkeeping.

harbor_master_oren.lore: Oren treats every clean docking and lawful departure as part of the work of keeping Valerion's western edge from going feral.

reactions.text: Oren gives you the courtesy usually reserved for escorts and officers. "Anyone the line trusts with D-rank work gets a straight answer here."

rumors.text: Stormbreak keeps morale by testing crews in friendly contests before the hard work begins again.

relic_dealer_nessa.name: Nessa Vey
relic_dealer_nessa.dialogue: Nessa Vey talks about relics with the caution of someone who has seen too many old protections sold by fools and bought by worse ones.

dialogue_set.default: Nessa Vey talks about relics with the caution of someone who has seen too many old protections sold by fools and bought by worse ones.

relic_dealer_nessa.lore: Nessa works the lawful side of relic salvage because she would rather see dangerous things cataloged than vanish into cult hands and rumor.

reactions.text: Nessa's eyes sharpen immediately. "Blackroot Ash Mark behind you? Then you've already seen the kind of relic work that ruins amateurs."

havik_ironborn.name: Havik Ironborn
havik_ironborn.dialogue: Havik Ironborn carries himself like a veteran who thinks clean patrol work matters more than loud stories, though he has enough scars to know the stories come anyway.

dialogue_set.default: Havik Ironborn carries himself like a veteran who thinks clean patrol work matters more than loud stories, though he has enough scars to know the stories come anyway.

havik_ironborn.lore: Based out of the Ironhold garrison, Havik treats monster work as one more line of frontier discipline rather than a separate trade.

reactions.text: Havik gives you a soldier's nod. "C-rank means the line can trust you with hard ground. I respect that."
Havik studies you for a beat, then inclines his head. "Anyone who broke Gorgos's hold on the pass has earned plain dealing from me."

rumors.text: Ironridge's worst hunts are never about glory. They are about making the road ordinary again.

dessa.name: Dessa
dessa.dialogue: Dessa wears confidence like polished armor and speaks as if every posted contract is already halfway hers unless somebody proves otherwise.

dialogue_set.default: Dessa wears confidence like polished armor and speaks as if every posted contract is already halfway hers unless somebody proves otherwise.

dessa.lore: Dessa built her name on fast contract clears around Stonewatch and has not yet decided whether you are competition or inconvenience.

reactions.text: Dessa's smile turns sharper but less dismissive. "Silver, then? Fine. That means I actually have to count you."

rumors.text: Dessa says the board only matters once the jobs start carrying marks that can actually make a name.

old_crane.name: Old Crane
old_crane.dialogue: Old Crane speaks like a man who has outlived too many confident hunters and sees no reason to rush a warning that will still be true tomorrow.

dialogue_set.default: Old Crane speaks like a man who has outlived too many confident hunters and sees no reason to rush a warning that will still be true tomorrow.

old_crane.lore: Crane drifts between boss marks and bad roads, arriving where hard hunts are about to teach the same old lesson to a new name.

reactions.text: Old Crane's eyes narrow with approval. "Good. Gorgos was the sort of brute who teaches bad habits if he lives too long."
Old Crane taps the haft of his spear once. "Alpha work changes how a hunter reads silence. Keep that lesson."

rumors.text: Old Crane warns that boss marks always start killing people long before the board learns the right name for them.

sable.name: Sable
sable.dialogue: Sable keeps her tone unreadable and her attention fixed on old wards, broken seals, and whether everyone nearby is pretending to know more than they do.

dialogue_set.default: Sable keeps her tone unreadable and her attention fixed on old wards, broken seals, and whether everyone nearby is pretending to know more than they do.

sable.lore: A Duskblood relic hunter with a habit of appearing wherever dangerous salvage starts attracting more ambition than discipline.

reactions.text: Sable studies you with fresh interest. "Blackroot Ash Mark behind you means you may actually understand what relic work costs."
Sable lets out a short breath. "So the Vault Keeper finally answered to somebody who lived. Useful to know."

rumors.text: Sable says relic hunters die fastest when they mistake an old ward for a dead one.

brother_aldun.name: Brother Aldun
brother_aldun.dialogue: Brother Aldun speaks softly enough that shrine bells seem louder around him, with the calm of someone who thinks hunting and prayer are both ways of keeping a line from failing.

dialogue_set.default: Brother Aldun speaks softly enough that shrine bells seem louder around him, with the calm of someone who thinks hunting and prayer are both ways of keeping a line from failing.

brother_aldun.lore: A Hollowborn hunter of shrine threats, Aldun treats every cleansed road and quieted altar as the same sacred duty carried forward.

reactions.text: Brother Aldun bows his head once. "Returning the sigil mattered. Too many hunters remember only how to take."
Aldun's tone grows warmer. "Bronze standing means the guild has begun to trust your judgment, not merely your blade."

rumors.text: Aldun says shrine hunts go bad the moment a hunter starts treating an old duty like easy salvage.

traveling_bard_kellan.name: Kellan Grey
traveling_bard_kellan.dialogue: Kellan Grey wears a road-dusted smile and listens like a collector of victories, failures, and the names people will be singing by next market day.

dialogue_set.default: Kellan Grey wears a road-dusted smile and listens like a collector of victories, failures, and the names people will be singing by next market day.

traveling_bard_kellan.lore: Kellan follows the held roads because songs move faster than caravans, and he intends to know which names the realm is already learning.

reactions.text: Kellan grins like he has found the ending of a verse. "So it was you at Ironridge. The guild halls are already ahead of me on that chorus."
